Smith identifies the **altar-angel-with-fire** as the executive-judgment messenger. The grapes fully ripe = the wicked filling up the cup of iniquity. The altar from which the angel proceeds is the altar of incense in the heavenly sanctuary (Rev 8:3-5).

Litch (per TMR 119.2): the altar-angel commanding the vintage signals the executive of the unmixed wrath; the grapes' ripeness is the cup-of-iniquity full. The literal plague-process described by Litch (boils, blood, scorching, darkness) is the **process of the trampling**.

Andrews: the altar-angel commanding the vintage = **the answer to the saints' cry under the altar** (Rev 6:9-10). The grapes are fully ripe = the wicked have completed their iniquity; the cup is full; divine wrath is justly poured out. The vintage executes the cup-of-fornication that Babylon made the nations drink (v. 8) — **lex talionis**.

Haskell: the altar-angel = the **executive of justice in response to the saints' martyrdom-cry** (Rev 6:9-10). The grapes' ripeness = the cup-of-iniquity is full; the time for mercy has passed; the time for execution has come.

modernReadings.citation: _Revelation's Seven Trumpets_, pp. 20-21; _Close of Probation_, p. 194
modernReadings.html: Bohr explicitly ties the altar-fire angel to Rev 8:5 / Rev 15-16: "**fire who pours out God's wrath into the winepress of His fury (Revelation 14:18)**" (_Seven Trumpets_, pp. 20-21). "**Jesus then harvests the wicked and throws them into the winepress outside spiritual Jerusalem (14:18, 19)**" (_Close of Probation_, p. 194). **Convergent on the altar-fire / executive-judgment chain.**

status.label: Miller-rule status
status.html: No violation. Convergent.

verses.text: And the angel thrust in his sickle into the earth, and gathered the vine of the earth, and cast it into the great winepress of the wrath of God.

pioneerReadings.html: EGW reads the winepress as the **executive judgment of the wicked**. Following Smith, EGW places this in the **post-millennial executive judgment** scenario (the wicked are gathered at the end of the 1000 years against the New Jerusalem and consumed by fire, GC 663-672) rather than at the Second Coming itself. The wine of wrath corresponds to the seventh plague (Rev 16:17-21) and the final fire of Rev 20:9-15.

Smith reads the winepress as the **post-millennial executive judgment** — the destruction of the wicked AFTER the 1000 years, at the descent of the New Jerusalem (Rev 20:9). Smith argues this from the chapter sequence (the vintage follows the harvest of the righteous, which is the Second Coming) but locates the trampling itself in the post-millennial scene of Rev 20:9 + Rev 19:11ff treated as the same complex of events: "**The wicked are gathered for destruction. The winepress of the wrath of God is the great consummating judgment on the wicked, which takes place at the close of the thousand years, when fire comes down from God out of heaven and devours them (Revelation 20:9). The winepress is trodden by the King of kings and Lord of lords with the armies of heaven (Revelation 19:11-21)**" (DAR 636.1, condensed).

Miller: the winepress = **the literal end-of-the-world judgment** at Christ's coming. Miller's premillennial framework does not separate Second-Coming from post-millennial executive judgment with the precision the later SDA writers developed.

Litch: the winepress = the **executive plague-process + Armageddon battle** (TMR 119.2 sequence). The gathering of the vine = the gathering of the wicked nations to the great battle; the casting into the winepress = the unmixed wrath outpouring.

Andrews maps the winepress to the **seven last plagues + Armageddon** (Rev 16-19), with the **horse-bridle blood** (v. 20) as the literal extent of the Armageddon slaughter. The vine = the wicked; the wrath of God = the unmixed plagues; the trampling = the divine execution. **Andrews diverges from Smith on timing**: Smith reads the winepress as **post-millennial** (after Rev 20); Andrews reads it as **at the Second Coming complex** (Armageddon, Rev 19:11-21).

Haskell: the vine of the earth = **the wicked harvest of the world**; the winepress = the **execution of divine wrath**. Haskell follows Andrews more than Smith on the timing — the winepress is part of the Second-Coming-Armageddon complex, not a separate post-millennial event.

modernReadings.citation: _From the Close of Probation to the New Earth_, pp. 70, 194; _Revelation's Seven Trumpets_, pp. 20-21
modernReadings.html: Bohr locates the winepress at the **pre-second-coming / Armageddon-seventh-plague** moment — when probation closes and the wicked are gathered for destruction at Christ's return, with Rev 19:11ff as the parallel. Verbatim: "**Revelation 14:14-20: When the two groups are complete, the seventh trumpet is about to sound**" (_Seven Trumpets_, pp. 357-358). "**Revelation 14:17-20: When probation closes, God's spiritual Israel (the harvest) is gathered inside worldwide spiritual Mt. Zion and the wicked spiritual Babylonians (the grapes) are gathered in the worldwide spiritual winepress outside the city**" (_Close of Probation_, p. 70). On the globalization of Joel 3: "**Revelation 14:14-17 globalizes Joel 3:9-12**" (_Close of Probation_, pp. 68-70). **Diverges from Smith on chronology**: Smith reads the trampling as **post-millennial** (Rev 20:9 cycle); Bohr reads it as **pre-second-coming / Armageddon** (the seventh plague / Rev 19:11ff at Christ's return, before the 1000 years).

status.label: Miller-rule status
status.html: **Bohr is marginal on Rule X (literal/figurative testing) and Rule XIII (typology must match prophetic chronology), but defensible on Rule V.** Smith's post-millennial reading follows the strict sequence Rev 19 (Second Coming + Armageddon) → Rev 20:1-3 (1000 years begin) → Rev 20:4-6 (saints reign with Christ) → Rev 20:7-10 (Satan loosed, wicked raised, fire from heaven) — the winepress trampling is the **Rev 20:9 event** read backward into Rev 14:19-20 via the Rev 19:15 same-symbol identification. Bohr's pre-coming reading collapses the Rev 19 / Rev 20:9 distinction into one Armageddon-Day-of-the-Lord event, treating the winepress as the seventh plague + Rev 19:11ff battle. The two readings yield identical theology (wicked destroyed, righteous vindicated) but **incompatible chronologies**: Smith places the trampling AFTER the 1000 years; Bohr places it BEFORE. EGW (GC 657-672) tends to read the winepress as **both** the second-coming destruction AND the post-millennial executive judgment (composite), which softens but does not fully resolve the divergence. This is the single most significant chronological recompose Bohr applies to Rev 14.

symbols.label: Symbols redefined
symbols.html: Winepress / vintage chronology (Smith: post-millennial — Rev 20:9; Bohr: pre-coming / Armageddon — Rev 19:11ff at the Second Coming, with the seventh-plague backdrop).

verses.text: And the winepress was trodden without the city, and blood came out of the winepress, even unto the horse bridles, by the space of a thousand and six hundred furlongs.

pioneerReadings.html: EGW: the winepress trodden **without the city** = the final destruction of the wicked **outside the New Jerusalem** after the millennium (cf. Rev 20:9). The 1600 furlongs of blood = the **literal extent of the executive judgment** (around 184 miles). EGW reads this scene with Smith as **post-millennial executive judgment**, distinct from the Second-Coming harvest.

Smith reads the **"without the city"** as outside the New Jerusalem after its descent (the Rev 20:9 setting — fire from heaven devouring the wicked surrounding the camp of the saints), and treats the 1600 furlongs as a literal or symbolic measure of the field of slaughter (Smith leans symbolic, citing the difficulty of forcing a strict literal application and the typological link to Isa 63:1-6 — Christ treading the winepress alone). "**The winepress is trodden without the city, that is, outside the New Jerusalem, which descends to the earth after the thousand years (Revelation 21:2)**" (DAR 636.2, condensed).

Miller: the winepress trodden without the city = **the literal destruction of the wicked at the Second Coming**. The 1600 furlongs Miller treats as a **literal Palestinian distance** marking the Armageddon battle-zone.

Litch: the winepress trodden without the city + the 1600 furlongs of blood = **literal Armageddon slaughter** with literal Palestinian geography. Convergent with Andrews/Haskell against Smith's post-millennial placement.
James White: the winepress outside the city = the **literal final destruction** of the wicked, parallel to Joel 3:12-16 and Rev 19:11-21.

Andrews: the winepress trodden without the city = the **Armageddon execution at the Second Coming complex** (Rev 16:14-16, 19:11-21). "_The coming of Christ as a thief does not take place until after the sixth vial is poured out. The gathering of the nations to the great battle, which is accomplished by the spirits, is brought to view in many scriptures_" (TMR 121.1). **1600 furlongs** = literal Palestinian geography of Armageddon (approx. length of Palestine). **Diverges from Smith and EGW** on timing: Andrews places the vintage at the Second Coming, not post-millennium.

Haskell: the winepress + 1600 furlongs = the **literal Armageddon battle** at the Second-Coming complex. The blood-up-to-horse-bridles language = the literal slaughter of the wicked kings and armies (Rev 19:17-21). The city excluded = Jerusalem (the historical type, parallel to the executive-judgment-outside-the-city of Joel 3).

modernReadings.citation: _From the Close of Probation to the New Earth_, p. 194
modernReadings.html: Bohr reads "without the city" as outside **spiritual Mt Zion** (the worldwide spiritual gathering of the remnant) — i.e., outside the spiritual city of God's people who are gathered inside at the Second Coming. Verbatim: "**Horses trample the winepress and blood splatters on the horses (14:20)**" (p. 194). On the inside/outside distinction: "**God's people are depicted as the harvest of the earth inside the city and the wicked are portrayed as the grapes gathered outside the city... Mount Zion here is the worldwide spiritual gathering of God's remnant people**" (p. 194). **Diverges from Smith on the city's identity**: Smith reads "the city" as the New Jerusalem post-descent (Rev 21:2); Bohr reads it as spiritual Mt Zion (the worldwide remnant gathered at the Second Coming, before the 1000 years).

status.label: Miller-rule status
status.html: **Same Rule-X / Rule-XIII marginal-violation issue as v. 19** — Bohr's spiritual-Mt-Zion-at-the-Second-Coming reading is the front end of his pre-millennial winepress chronology. The Rev 19:11ff parallel (where the King of kings rides with the armies of heaven and treads the winepress at the Second Coming) supports Bohr; the strict Rev 19 → Rev 20 sequence supports Smith. The 1600-furlong measure neither reading treats with strict literalness; both read it symbolically (universality of the slaughter / 4 × 4 × 100 completeness number).

symbols.label: Symbols redefined
symbols.html: The city (Smith: New Jerusalem post-descent — Rev 21:2; Bohr: spiritual Mt Zion at the Second Coming — Rev 14:1's worldwide remnant gathering).

summaryHtml:
**Rev 14 violation summary (against _From the Close of Probation to the New Earth_, _Revelation's Seven Trumpets_, _Revelation's Seven Seals_, and _Studies on Daniel 11_):** Of 20 verses, Bohr has Rule-X / Rule-XIII marginal violations on **2** (vv. 19, 20 — the post-millennial-vs-pre-coming winepress chronology, the single most significant chronological recompose in Bohr's Rev 14 treatment). The chapter's core agrees with pioneer on every major identification: Mt Zion = the redeemed; 144,000 = the last-generation translated saints; three angels = three successive end-time messages (first = 1844 hour-of-judgment / worship-the-Creator-Sabbath; second = Babylon fallen, 1844 + future Loud Cry; third = mark-of-beast / Sabbath-Sunday warning); v. 13 special blessing from 1844 (with Smith-footnote special resurrection that Bohr expands into a 6-page treatment); Son of man on the cloud = Christ at the Second Coming; harvest of vv. 14-16 = righteous ingathered; vintage of vv. 17-20 = wicked destroyed. Bohr's distinctive extensions are: (a) the **four-portraits framework** of the 144,000 (Rev 7 + 14 + 15 + 19 read as one composite — a Rule-V harmonization, not a violation); (b) the **1798 starting bracket** for the first angel (Rev 10 unsealing → Rev 14:6 proclamation — defensible Rule-V chain); (c) the **two-stage second angel** (1844 + future Loud Cry — convergent with EGW on Rev 18:1 swelling cry); (d) the **spiritual-Mt-Zion-as-worldwide-remnant** reading where Smith leaves Mt Zion as the heavenly locale (marginal — see vv. 19, 20). On v. 13 Bohr is _more_ developed than Smith (the 6-page special-resurrection treatment expands what Smith handles in a footnote). Verses where Bohr offers no discrete verse-by-verse exegesis but the substance is covered under larger blocks: vv. 11 (annihilationist torment) covered implicitly under the third-angels' executive-judgment block.

---
